reasoning: India's total fertility rate (TFR) has already fallen to 2.0 as of the most recent World Bank estimates (2021/2022) and is estimated at 1.98 for 2024 by the UN World Population Prospects (WPP). Extensive demographic analyses anticipate a continued steady decline as India continues to develop and urbanize, with UN projections anticipating a TFR around 1.81-1.83 by 2032-2033. It is incredibly rare for countries that have reached replacement-level fertility to reverse course and shoot back above 2.0 over the subsequent decade. Given the current sub-2.0 baseline and strong structural drivers keeping fertility low, the probability that the 2032-2033 average TFR falls below 2.0 is exceptionally high. The community prediction of 98% is well-founded, and I place my probabilities in the 95-97% range to account for only the most marginal tail risks (e.g. dramatic data methodology revisions).
